data.title: Why a Django reader?
data.excerpt: Python runs the data web. Django is the dominant Python framework. Here's why @webhouse/cms ships a first-class Django reader.
data.content:
## The Python audience

Python is the most-used language in the world according to TIOBE and IEEE Spectrum. Django powers Instagram, Disqus, Mozilla, Pinterest, and most of the data-engineering web. ML/AI teams live in Python — and they often need a content layer for documentation, marketing, and dashboards.

## Why a reader, not an API?

A file reader is just `Path.glob()` and `json.loads()`. It's instant. It works offline. It's testable with regular `pytest` fixtures. And it's a pattern Python developers already know from `pathlib`.

## What's in the package

- `webhouse.collection(name, locale)` — list published documents
- `webhouse.document(collection, slug)` — load one document
- `webhouse.find_translation(doc, collection)` — sibling via translationGroup
- `webhouse.globals_doc()` — cached singleton for site-wide settings
- Path traversal protection via `SAFE_NAME` regex + resolved-path prefix check

Django integration:

- `blog.context_processors.webhouse_globals` — inject globals into every template
- `blog.templatetags.webhouse_filters.wh_string` — `{{ post|wh_string:"title" }}` filter

That's it. ~120 lines of Python. Zero deps beyond Django itself + `markdown` for richtext rendering.

## The result

Your Django app reads the same JSON files that CMS admin writes. Editors get a beautiful visual editor. You keep Django.
data.author: Christian Broberg
